content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Targeted planned-elsewhere and success-reconciliation tests pass, but I found two correctness issues in the new recovery flow: duplicate comments are still possible when verified-state persistence fails, and reconstruction across multiple aggregates can emit the same mapping multiple times.

## Amendments

1. `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:772-813` still posts mapped-bead comments even when writing the verified mapping fails at `:782-788`. If `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping(...)` returns an error, phase 3 still calls `comment_bead(...)`, so a retry/replay sees no durable `mapped_bead_verified=true` record and can post the same comment again. Only mappings whose verified state was durably recorded should advance to comment posting.
   Rationale: The change is trying to close the duplicate-comment crash window, but the remaining persistence-failure path still breaks idempotency and can spam the mapped bead with repeated comments.
2. `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:883-948` deduplicates reconstructed mappings only against the journal snapshot loaded at entry. Because `existing_keys` is never updated after a new mapping is reconstructed, the same `(finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id)` present in multiple final-review aggregates will be reconstructed once per aggregate, then verified/commented once per copy in the same reconciliation run. Insert each newly reconstructed identity into the dedup set before continuing.
   Rationale: Completion rounds can legitimately repeat the same planned-elsewhere finding. In the recovery case this code now emits duplicate journal rows and duplicate `br` comments for one logical mapping.
